title: bruniaceae
intro: The bruniaceae rank is a family of flowering plants that consists of approximately 60 species of shrubs and small trees found in the southern hemisphere, particularly in Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa. These plants are generally evergreen and produce small, white or yellow flowers. The bruniaceae family is part of the broader group of flowering plants that have five-petaled flowers and are primarily insect-pollinated. Members of the bruniaceae family are often used in landscaping and horticulture due to their attractive foliage and fragrant flowers.
